---
title: "Qualcomm NRE Spend Forecasting"
description: "Predictive analytics model to forecast Non-Recurring Engineering spend using Python and Databricks"
categories: [Python, Databricks, Predictive Modeling, Forecasting]
date: "2026-06-01"
image: "../../files/qualcomm_thumb.png"
---

## Overview

This capstone project was developed in collaboration with **Qualcomm** to address a core challenge in engineering budget planning: forecasting Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) spend with enough accuracy to support strategic workforce and financial decisions.

## Problem Statement

Qualcomm's engineering leadership needed a data-driven way to project NRE costs across product roadmaps and workforce plans. Without reliable forecasts, budget accuracy and scenario planning were limited by manual estimation.

## Approach

- Integrated **labor, roadmap, and finance data** from multiple enterprise systems into a unified modeling pipeline
- Built predictive analytics models in **Python on Azure Databricks** to forecast NRE spend at the program and organizational level
- Designed scenario-based projections enabling engineering leadership to model "what-if" planning assumptions

## Key Contributions

| Area | Details |
|------|---------|
| Data Integration | Unified multi-source enterprise data across finance, HR, and roadmap systems |
| Modeling | Developed regression and time-series models for spend forecasting |
| Deployment | Pipeline designed for reproducibility in Databricks environment |
| Business Impact | Supports workforce planning and budget accuracy for engineering leadership |

## Tech Stack

`Python` · `Azure Databricks` · `Pandas` · `Scikit-learn` · `Predictive Modeling` · `Apache Spark`

## Status

🚧 **In Progress** — Expected completion: June 2026 (Capstone)
